preview_text:
The model favors Senegal at 61%, with Iraq's win probability at 12%. The sportsbook consensus pushes Senegal notably higher to 73%, widening the gap to 12 percentage points in the favorite's direction.

The model sees a reasonably competitive match: a draw is plausible at 27%, suggesting meaningful uncertainty in the outcome. The 35-point spread between Senegal and Iraq indicates a clear lean but not a blowout expectation. The presence of draw probability at more than one-quarter reflects the contingency typical of knockout-stage football on neutral ground.

Model and market diverge meaningfully here. The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices both assign Senegal closer to 73–75%, substantially above the model's 61%. The books also compress the draw to 17–16%, roughly 10 points below the model. This dashboard does not adjudicate which source better reflects the true probabilities; the spread suggests material disagreement on Senegal's strength relative to Iraqi chances and the likelihood of a stalemate.
